description_en: The Chief Data Officer Branch (CDOB), Employment Insurance (EI) Benefits Delivery Services (BDS) and Innovation, Information and Technology Branch (IITB) are implementing an artificial intelligence (AI)/machine learning solution that will interpret and assess free text comments captured by employers when records of employment (ROE) are issued. Based on very specific business rules, the AI will assess and predict simple actions (i.e. save or ignore comments, predict a different Reason for Separation [RFS]). The actions taken following an AI prediction will be saved within the internal interpretative notes used in the process of calculating EI claims. The original document (Record of Employment [ROE]) will not be modified.
description_fr: La Direction générale de la dirigeante principale des données (DGDPD), les Services de versement des prestations (SVP) de l’assurance-emploi (AE) et la Direction générale de l’innovation, de l’information et de la technologie (DGIIT) mettent en oeuvre une solution d’intelligence artificielle (IA) et d'apprentissage automatique qui interprétera et évaluera les commentaires en texte libre saisis par les employeurs quand les relevés d’emploi (RE) sont émis. Selon des règles opérationnelles très précises, l’IA évaluera et prévoira des mesures simples (c.-à-d. enregistrer ou ignorer les commentaires ou prévoir un motif de cessation d’emploi [MCE] différent). Les mesures prises à la suite de prédictions de l’IA seront enregistrées dans les notes interprétatives internes utilisées dans le traitement des demandes d'AE. Le document initial (RE) ne sera pas modifié.
_raw_data_json: {"projectDetailsRespondent": "Jeff Hughes", "projectDetailsDepartment-NS": "014", "projectDetailsTitle": "Record of Employments (ROE) Comments Assessment ", "projectDetailsPhase": "item2", "projectDetailsDescription": "The Chief Data Officer Branch (CDOB), Employment Insurance (EI) Benefits Delivery Services (BDS) and Innovation, Information and Technology Branch (IITB) are implementing an artificial intelligence (AI)/machine learning solution that will interpret and assess free text comments captured by employers when records of employment (ROE) are issued. Based on very specific business rules, the AI will assess and predict simple actions (i.e. save or ignore comments, predict a different Reason for Separation [RFS]). The actions taken following an AI prediction will be saved within the internal interpretative notes used in the process of calculating EI claims. The original document (Record of Employment [ROE]) will not be modified.", "businessDrivers1": ["item3", "item5"], "businessDrivers3": "The system will assist in meeting service standards for benefit delivery by reducing the need for manual inspection of text comments in ROE forms. The system will be able to handle simple cases, leaving the more complex cases for human agents. Handling simple cases reduces the workload, freeing up agents' time for other aspects of EI benefits delivery, which may result in faster service delivery.", "businessDrivers4": "As the system will free up the time of human agents to focus on other aspects of EI benefits delivery, the public may benefit from faster service delivery, which has a general economic benefit. The cost savings of the system, allowing for more effective stewardship of public funds, is also a public benefit.", "businessDrivers5": "item3-0", "businessDrivers6": "This system will be limited in scope to the examination of text comments in one field of the ROE form. Manual inspection of this field is time-consuming and yet often yields no relevant information, or information that is redundant with other areas of the ROE (Reason for Separation [RFS] section). We estimate that this AI system will be able to eliminate about 30-40% of work items related to manual inspection of text comments. This would work out, very roughly, to a cost savings of about $200k to $300k per year.", "businessDrivers9": "item1-0", "businessDrivers11": ["item5-0"], "businessDrivers7": "The system only takes the text comment and the Reason for Separation (RFS) and Detail (RFS-DTL) codes as input; it does not examine any other portion of the ROE. There are checks in place to ensure that more complex cases are left untouched, and to ensure that the model is confident enough in its prediction before any action is taken. The model is designed to operate similarly to how human agents process ROE text comments, so the scope of the model is limited and designed to fit within the existing workflow, resulting in no changes to EI eligibility criteria or decision-making processes.", "businessDrivers8": "Client interests and program objectives are largely aligned in this context, in that both are interested in ensuring decisions are made quickly and correctly. The AI model will not be making decisions about eligibility, only about information contained in one portion of the ROE form. It is also set up to limit negative consequences of model errors; consideration was given to the full EI workflow with respect to when human intervention occurs. The system cannot make an error that would result in benefits erroneously being denied, without human intervention later in the process. Thus, the system is intended to improve the speed of service delivery, and minimize risk of error that would negatively impact clients.", "riskProfile1": "item2-0", "riskProfile2": "item2-0", "riskProfile3": "item2-0", "riskProfile4": "item2-0", "riskProfile5": "item2-0", "projectAuthority1": "item2-0", "aboutSystem1": ["item2"], "aboutAlgorithm1": "item2-0", "aboutAlgorithm2": "item1-3", "decisionSector1": ["item3-1"], "impact30": "item2-2", "impact3": "item1-4", "impact20": "The system evaluates the text information provided in the open comment field of the ROE, and attempts to classify it with respect to any information it may contain regarding a Reason for Separation (RFS). This system is a machine learning model that was trained on past ROE comments, to learn patterns associated with RFS information. The criteria involved include whether there is any information present about a reason for separation, and whether the existing RFS code provided on the ROE form is consistent with that information. No other information on the ROE is used in the evaluation.", "impact21": "The system produces a prediction about the RFS information contained in the comment (if any). Additional business logic determines whether the RFS code should be adjusted to be in line with the information in the comment. The administrative decisions made regarding EI eligibility are made, in part, on the basis of the RFS code, for which information is provided on the EI Program's website. The criteria for EI eligibility will not change as a result of the automated system.", "impact22": "item2-0", "impact5": "item1-4", "impact6": "item1-1", "impact7": "item1-1", "impact8": "Information received on an ROE can normally be used on an EI claim for a period of one year, excluding certain exceptions where the period is extended and possible appeals. The impacts are reversible because the system is only addressing Reason for Separation (RFS) information in ROE text comments; administration and fact-finding steps from Service Canada agents later in the process could result in further adjustments to the RFS. The system does not make decisions about EI eligibility.", "impact9": "item1-1", "impact24": "item1-1", "impact25": "The system is designed to use minimal information in order to respect privacy and reduce the risk of unequal treatment. In addition, a fairness assessment was undertaken to evaluate the model, to ensure that the actions taken by the model are fair and unbiased across a range of sociodemographic groups.", "impact11": "item1-1", "impact12": "The AI decision has no relation, hence no impacts, to the health and well-being of individuals.", "impact13": "item1-1", "impact14": "As it is completely reversible, the AI decision may only result in minor impacts to the speed at which the claim can be processed, if additional verification is required on a specific decision made. In the vast majority of cases, the processing speed will be unchanged or increased.", "impact15": "item1-1", "aboutDataSource1": "item1-4", "aboutDataSource9": "item1-0", "aboutDataSource10": "item2-0", "aboutDataSource11": "item1-0", "aboutDataSource12": "ESDC PPU 385", "aboutDataSource2": "item4-3", "aboutDataSource3": "item2-1", "aboutDataSource4": "item2-0", "aboutDataSource5": "item2-0", "aboutDataSource6": "item1-4", "aboutDataSource7": "item1-1", "aboutDataSource8": "item1-1", "aboutDataType1": "item1", "aboutDataType2": ["item1-2"], "consultationImplementation1": "item1-1", "consultationImplementation2": ["item2", "item3", "item4", "item5", "item6", "item13", "item10"], "consultationImplementation3": "item2-0", "dataQualityImplementation1": "item1-2", "dataQualityImplementation2": "item1-1", "dataQualityImplementation3": "item1-1", "dataQualityImplementation4": "item2-0", "dataQualityImplementation5": "item1-1", "dataQualityImplementation6": "item2-0", "dataQualityImplementation7": "item1-2", "dataQualityImplementation8": "item1-2", "dataQualityImplementation9": "item2-0", "dataQualityImplementation10": "item2-0", "fairnessImplementation1": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation2": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation3": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation4": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation5": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation6": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation7": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation8": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation9": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation10": "item2-0", "fairnessImplementation11": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation12": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation13": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation14": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation15": "item1-1", "fairnessImplementation16": "item1-2", "fairnessImplementation17": "item2-0", "privacyImplementation1": "item1-1", "privacyImplementation2": "item1-1", "privacyImplementation3": "item1-1", "privacyImplementation4": "item1-1", "privacyImplementation7": "item1-1", "impact10": "As the prediction made by the AI is used in support of a larger eligibility decisions, and is reversible, the impacts resulting from the decisions have little to no impact on the rights or freedoms of the individual applying for EI benefits.", "impact16": "The decision will have very little impacts, if any, on the sustainability of an environmental ecosystem.", "businessDrivers10": "The open text field in the ROE form is necessary for the provision of important additional information to provide context for an EI claim; removing it is not an option. Thus, it either needs to be reviewed manually, an automated system must be used, or some combination of the two. The preferred option, combining an automated system and human review, will reduce the workload for simple cases so human agents can spend their time on more complex cases.", "decisionSector3": "The model will only take action on comments related to simple decisions, which are based on current procedures used by agents. These decisions involve determining if the comment contains information relevant to the Reason for Separation (RFS), and adjusting the RFS if necessary. When the AI interpretation is used in a decision, it will replace a decision currently made by a human that requires minimal judgement and/or discretion. However, a portion of the decisions (ones which cannot be assessed by AI, or more complex decisions) will continue to be made by humans.", "impact31": "When the AI system takes actions, it will adjust the Reason for Separation (RFS) in accordance with information in the text comment. This RFS code is used as part of the administrative decision-making process for determining eligibility for EI benefits. The AI system itself does not make any decisions about EI claims. In cases where the AI system does not take action, the text comment will be left for a human agent to inspect, and no changes will be made to the RFS.", "privacyImplementation8": "Data are de-identified when provided to the AI system by removing personal information such as name, Social Insurance Number, and employer information. Outputs are similarly stored in a de-identified manner as well. Outputs are retained for a minimum of 12 years to align with the existing ROE retention period, deleted only when the ROE itself is deleted.", "projectDetailsJob": "Senior Data Scientist", "projectDetailsBranch": "CDOB", "aboutDataSource13": "The input data is provided by Record of Employment (ROE) forms submitted to ESDC by employers. The data used for training the system was collected from the administrative database which stores these ROE forms when they are submitted.", "privacyImplementation5": "A Privacy Impact Assessment, entitled \"Record of Employment-Comment Artificial Intelligence Model\" was completed April 2021. The scope of the privacy assessment included the evaluation of the security and safeguards related to the technologies used, including encryption and access controls; the collection, use, and retention of personal information; accuracy and monitoring of the automated solution; accountability for the solution; and procedures related to individuals' right to access their own personal information and file complaints or inquiries about policies and practices related to handling of this information. The assessment was specifically focused on the implementation of the automated system, not on the broader EI program and existing processes."}
